title: Directional Expansion Readiness Assessment

prototypeScopeVerification.basis: V684-V710 artifacts are explicitly named and scoped to the US 59 / I-69 corridor inventory; V695 uses that recovered confidence artifact for candidate generation.
validated: incident-context readiness for the prototype corridor
prototypeScopeVerification.v710FindingClassification: Expected prototype behavior, not evidence of an unexpected coverage failure

currentDirectionalCoverage.coverageConcentration: Current validated coverage is concentrated in the I-69 / US 59 prototype corridor.

expansionCandidateInventory.containmentStatus: Prototype-contained for extracted inventory; includes county attribution across Montgomery, Liberty, San Jacinto, Harris, and missing-county review records
expansionCandidateInventory.productionReadiness: Ready with observations for offline incident-context enrichment only; not runtime-connected
Not ready until extraction, containment, confidence, and incident-context review run
Not ready until extraction, containment, confidence, and incident-context review run
Not ready until extraction, containment, confidence, and incident-context review run
Not ready until extraction, containment, confidence, and incident-context review run
Not ready until extraction, containment, confidence, and incident-context review run
Not ready until extraction, containment, confidence, and incident-context review run

countyReadiness.currentDirectionalCoverage: 28 strong prototype-corridor candidates and 3 construction review records in the extracted I-69 / US 59 inventory
74 strong prototype-corridor candidates, 17 manual-review records, and 1 missing-oneway review record
reviewRequiredCorridors: I-69 / US 59 manual-review and missing-oneway records

countyReadiness.currentDirectionalCoverage: 1 strong prototype-corridor candidate and 4 construction review records

multiCountyScalability.containmentReadiness: Ready with observations; county leakage and missing county records remain review exclusions
multiCountyScalability.confidenceModelReadiness: Ready with observations; model is corridor-agnostic but empirically validated only on I-69 / US 59
multiCountyScalability.operationalScalability: Not ready for production operation without per-corridor extraction and review runs
multiCountyScalability.maintenanceBurden: Moderate now; high if each county/corridor expansion is maintained manually

confidenceExpansionReadiness.canSupportAdditionalCorridorsToday: Yes, as an offline assessment model
confidenceExpansionReadiness.rationale: The model can score additional extracted records, but non-motorway FM/SH corridors likely need calibration for bidirectional roads, missing one-way semantics, and less complete lane/destination metadata.

futureReviewCandidates: missing_county where county can be independently resolved
missing_oneway on clearly bidirectional rural corridors

expansionOrderRecommendation.rationale: Highest county-wide awareness value and a named Liberty baseline corridor from prior county-platform defaults; use as the first non-prototype arterial validation.
Adds major north-south/state-highway and commuter arterial patterns after US 90 validates non-prototype extraction.
Adds lower-order or more localized corridors after confidence behavior is tuned for state/FM road classes.

corridors: Additional protected corridors identified during county onboarding
expansionOrderRecommendation.rationale: Future counties should onboard only after the first three phases prove repeatable extraction, containment, review, and confidence operations.

productionImpactProjection.rationale: The current prototype alone is corridor-concentrated; adding US 90, TX 146, TX 321, FM 1960, FM 1409, and FM 1011 would materially expand named-road incident matchability, but area-only/weather-only incidents and review-excluded records would remain ineligible.

supportingEvidence: Current validated candidates exist only for the prototype I-69 / US 59 inventory.
No requested expansion corridors are present in extracted directional inventory today.
Architecture and confidence model are designed to scale offline, but evidence must be generated per corridor before production use.
Review buckets remain intentionally blocked and unchanged.
